what_it_does: Defines responsive imagery handling: aspect-ratio tokens (square through ultrawide plus hero), image radius/background/overlay tokens, a `.media` ratio-locked box with object-fit children, a legibility `.scrim` gradient, a `.img-blurup` blur-up loading treatment, and a `[data-art]` art-direction system that shows the crop matching the declared `[data-surface]`.
observations: Aspect-ratio tokens: --ar-square 1/1, portrait 3/4, landscape 4/3, wide 16/9, ultrawide 21/9, hero 3/2
.media locks aspect-ratio (default --ar-wide) with overflow hidden to prevent layout shift while loading
.media children (img/video/image-slot) absolutely fill and use object-fit var(--fit, cover) and var(--pos, center)
.scrim adds a bottom-up linear-gradient ::after for text legibility over imagery
.img-blurup starts blurred + scaled and transitions to sharp when .is-loaded is added by JS on load
Art direction: [data-art] hidden by default; surface-scoped selectors reveal desktop/portrait/landscape crops; fallback shows desktop crop when no surface declared
References custom element <image-slot> as a possible media child
standouts: Art-direction via [data-surface] descendant selectors as a 'framed-preview' alternative to native <picture> media queries
Zero-layout-shift framing through aspect-ratio plus absolute-fill children
conventions: @kind other annotations on the aspect-ratio token values
Reuses motion tokens (--dur-enter, --ease-out) for the blur-up transition
concerns: The blur-up transition references --ease-out, but the motion file (motion.css) defines --ease-entrance/--ease-exit/--ease-emphasized/--ease-spring — no --ease-out is defined there, so the easing may fall back to default unless --ease-out exists in another token file (unverified from this file alone)
notes: The single home for ratio-locked media boxes, blur-up loading, legibility scrims, and surface-keyed art direction; default ratio is 16/9 and default fit is cover.
questions: Is --ease-out defined elsewhere, or is the blur-up easing relying on a token that does not exist (the motion file uses different easing names)?
What is the <image-slot> custom element and where is it defined/registered?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — header promises responsive, no-flash images with art direction; the file delivers ratio boxes, blur-up, scrim, and surface-keyed crop switching.
intention_signals: Trying to make imagery responsive and stable by construction — never flashing or shifting layout, cropping appropriately per surface, and resolving from blurred placeholder to sharp.
novelty: The [data-surface]-scoped art-direction selectors as a CSS-only crop switcher distinct from <picture>, and the <image-slot> custom element as a media child.
contribution: Contributes the aspect-ratio token set, the ratio-locked .media box, blur-up loading, the legibility scrim, and the surface-keyed art-direction mechanism.
summary_for_embedding: ConceptV design-system CSS for responsive imagery: aspect-ratio tokens, a layout-shift-free .media box, blur-up loading, a legibility scrim, and surface-keyed art-direction crop switching.
intention_for_embedding: To make images responsive and non-flashing by construction, with per-surface crops and progressive blur-up loading driven by single-source tokens.
